commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers combine provider-bound serialization safety with missing contributor real-behavior proof, which automation cannot supply for the contributor’s setup.; Needs attention: the patch expands provider-bound serialization of arbitrary structured tool results and current head lacks bounded, text-first replay safety.; Review finding: [P1] Bound structured fallback... (sha=04b044826a206e957449307bc87744b191f86bf8)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a shared provider-side tool-result text extractor and wires it into OpenAI Responses, OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Google/Gemini, Mistral, and transport regression tests.

PR surface: Source +134, Tests +484. Total +618 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main filters affected provider/transport tool-result replay down to explicit text blocks, and the linked issue has live Discord and Telegram reports of text/JSON output degrading to the placeholder. I did not run a live current-main session repro in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers combine provider-bound serialization safety with missing contributor real-behavior proof, which automation cannot supply for the contributor’s setup.

target.title: fix(llm): preserve structured tool result text across providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where agent sessions could receive literal `(see attached image)` placeholders instead of normal text/JSON tool results when a tool result block was structured but not typed as `text`.

This replacement PR covers the provider conversion paths from #96895 and also covers the active OpenAI Responses transport path noted in the review there.

Closes #96857
Related: #96895

## Why This Change Was Made

Tool-result replay code in multiple provider/transport paths only collected blocks with `type: "text"`. Structured non-image blocks could therefore be treated as empty output and collapse to an image/no-output fallback. This change adds a bounded shared extractor for structured non-image tool-result blocks and uses it in OpenAI Responses, OpenAI-compatible chat completions, Google/Gemini conversion, and the OpenAI Responses transport converter.

The current `main` fallback behavior is preserved:

- empty non-image output remains `(no output)` where that fallback already exists;
- image-only output remains `(see attached image)`;
- explicit text blocks remain text-first and are not mixed with structured fallback JSON;
- structured non-media output is serialized as fallback text only when no explicit text block is available.

## Review Updates In Current Head

Current head: `58572eddc07d2e93920bffe6ed22d10759039664`.

This update addresses the latest review findings by:

- restoring text-first structured fallback semantics: explicit text blocks win, and structured blocks are only used as fallback when no explicit text is present;
- adding mixed text-plus-structured regression coverage;
- skipping media-only structured blocks with `type: "image"`, `type: "image_url"`, or `type: "audio"` during provider text extraction;
- omitting MIME-tagged binary `data` before provider/model replay, including `mime_type`, `mediaType`, and `content_type` aliases;
- narrowing data URI redaction to actual inline data URI values while preserving ordinary prose such as `metadata:ready` and `data: is ordinary prose`;
- redacting sensitive structured fields before provider replay;
- omitting opaque or binary structured fields before provider replay;
- adding focused regression coverage in `src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts`.

## User Impact

Agents should be able to inspect structured text-like tool output in their model context instead of losing it to an attachment placeholder. Existing text output remains preferred, and media/binary payloads do not become provider-bound JSON text.

## Evidence

- Live issue evidence: #96857 includes current redacted observations where ordinary text/status tool outputs reached an agent-visible context as literal `(see attached image)` placeholders instead of text.
- Code-path evidence: current `main` filtered tool-result content down to `type: "text"` in the affected provider/transport paths; this patch routes those paths through one bounded structured-text extractor.
- Review-finding coverage in current head `58572eddc07d2e93920bffe6ed22d10759039664`:
  - `src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts` covers text-first mixed text+structured behavior.
  - The same test file covers media-only `image` / `image_url` / `audio` blocks and verifies they do not become provider replay text.
  - The same test file covers mixed media plus structured non-media content and verifies the structured non-media content remains fallback replay text.
  - The same test file covers MIME-tagged binary `data` omission, ordinary data-colon prose preservation, actual inline data URI redaction, and sensitive/opaque/binary field handling.
- Redacted terminal proof command:

```text
node --experimental-strip-types /tmp/pr97742-provider-replay-proof.ts
```

Redacted proof excerpt:

```text
CASE text-first mixed text+structured: VISIBLE_TEXT
CASE structured fallback without text: {"type":"json","value":{"ok":true,"count":2}}
CASE media-only blocks skipped: <empty>
CASE MIME-tagged binary omitted: {"type":"resource","mime_type":"application/octet-stream","data":"[binary data omitted: 12 chars]"}
CASE ordinary data-colon prose preserved: {"type":"json","value":{"note":"metadata:ready","text":"data: is ordinary prose"}}
CASE actual inline data URI redacted: {"type":"json","value":{"preview":"thumbnail=[inline data URI: 23 chars] done"}}
```

- Local pre-check only on current head:
  - `git diff --check` completed without whitespace errors.
  - `node --experimental-strip-types --check src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.ts` completed successfully.
  - `node --experimental-strip-types --check src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts` completed successfully.
  - `node --experimental-strip-types /tmp/pr97742-provider-replay-proof.ts` completed successfully and produced the redacted proof transcript above.
- GitHub Actions validation: rerunning for current head after this update. Previous head passed `Real behavior proof`, `check-lint`, `check-prod-types`, `check-test-types`, and `check-guards`; this section should be refreshed with current-head run links after Actions settle.

## Risk

- Message-delivery behavior changes are intentionally limited to structured non-media tool-result blocks that were previously dropped by text-only filtering.
- Explicit text remains preferred over structured fallback text, preserving existing replay semantics.
- Media-only blocks remain excluded from text extraction, so image/audio payloads keep using existing media handling paths instead of becoming provider-bound JSON text.
- MIME-tagged binary data, opaque fields, and sensitive fields are omitted or redacted before provider replay.
- Structured fallback blocks are serialized with bounded length and circular-reference handling to avoid unbounded replay text.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore the previous per-provider text-only extraction behavior. No migration, dependency, workflow, or persistent data changes are included.

## Not Tested / Limitations

- No live external provider request was made by this update.
- The redacted proof above is a terminal/helper replay transcript plus affected call-site proof, not a live external-provider transcript.
target.author: snowzlmbot

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
